about.title: About FOSS4G
about.teaser: Join us on 25 September 2025 in Brussels for a one-day conference of keynotes, hands-on workshops, and community networking around open-source geospatial tools.

whoWeAre.title: Who We Are
whoWeAre.content: We are the Belgian chapter of the Open Source Geospatial Foundation (OSGeo).

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.content: The OSGeo Foundation is a non-profit organization whose mission is to support the development of open-source geospatial software and promote its use. The foundation provides financial, organizational, and legal support to the broader open-source geospatial community.

support.title: Support
support.content: Our organization serves as an independent legal entity where members can contribute code, funding, and other resources, ensuring the preservation of contributions for public benefit. OSGeo also acts as an outreach and advocacy organization, providing a forum and shared infrastructure to strengthen collaboration among projects.

events.title: FOSS4G Belgium Events
events.content: One of the main activities of OSGeo Belgium is organizing events that bring together the open-source geospatial community in Belgium. Our flagship event is FOSS4G Belgium.

projects.title: Open Source Projects
projects.content: The projects supported by the OSGeo Foundation are all freely available and usable under an OSI-certified open-source license. These projects include popular geospatial software such as QGIS, GeoServer, PostGIS, and many others that are widely used in the industry.

contact.askByEmailTo: For any inquiries, you can email us at

callToPresentations.title: Call for Presentations
callToPresentations.pleaseSubmit: Be part of the success of the 2025 edition. Submit your proposal!

overview.title: About
overview.content1: We cover a wide range of audiences and topics related to geomatics: tech, geek, public use cases, private or professional.
overview.content2: Our goal? A conference rich in diversity, both in topics and speakers.

considerations.title: Some considerations
considerations.duration: Duration: 25 min (20 min presentation + 5 min Q&A).
considerations.explain: Clearly explain what you want to share.
considerations.message: Focus on the message and the main technology.
considerations.passion: Make your talk exciting, then continue the discussion over a drink.

topics.title: Topic ideas
topics.whatsNew: What's new in the OSGeo world?
topics.tools: Open source GIS: QGIS, PostGIS, GDAL, Proj…
topics.webdata: Storage, processing and dissemination of web spatial data
topics.use: Use by governments, businesses and researchers
topics.migration: Migration from commercial to open source solutions
topics.institutions: Open source solutions for Belgian institutions

submission.title: Format & Submission
submission.presentations: Presentations in English, Dutch, or French (20 min + 5 min Q&A).

criterias.title: Deadline and Selection Criteria
criterias.deadlineContent: 8 August 2025 – submit as soon as possible.
criterias.criteriasContent: Quality, relevance to the audience, and fit within the program.

volunteer.title: Looking for Volunteers
volunteer.content: We are looking for volunteers to help us organize FOSS4G Belgium. If you are interested, please contact us to join the team and contribute to the success of the event.

callForTopics.title: Call for Presentations
callForTopics.description: Accepting presentations on open-source geospatial topics. (Deadline: Friday, 8 August 2025)

callForSponsors.title: Call for Sponsors
callForSponsors.description: Support FOSS4G Belgium 2025 and gain visibility within the open-source geospatial community.

volunteers.title: Volunteer with Us
volunteers.description: Lend your skills and energy to help make FOSS4G Belgium 2025 a success.

hero.title: Call for Sponsors
hero.description: We are seeking sponsors to support this fantastic event.

support.title: Support FOSS4G Belgium 2025
support.description: Gain visibility within the open-source geospatial community by sponsoring our flagship event.

footer.subtext: Your support makes FOSS4G Belgium 2025 possible!
